name: APERT SYNDROME

demographics.Etiology: autosomal dominant with incomplete penetrance; sporadic (in majority)

dominant_finding: brachycephalic skull (← coronal craniosynostosis) + flat occiput
distribution: widened metopic + sagittal sutures extending from glabella to posterior fontanel (closing
discriminating_features: brachycephalic skull (← coronal craniosynostosis) + flat occiput
widened metopic + sagittal sutures extending from glabella to posterior fontanel (closing

general: brachycephalic skull (← coronal craniosynostosis) + flat occiput
widened metopic + sagittal sutures extending from glabella to posterior fontanel (closing
prominent vertical crest in middle of forehead (← increased intracranial pressure)
V-shaped anterior fossa ← elevation of lateral margins of lesser sphenoid
cervical spine fusion (in up to 71%), commonly of 5th and 6th vertebrae
Hand & feet: severe symmetric syndactyly = fusion of distal portions of phalanges, metacarpals /
